questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The correct statement(s) concerning the structures $P, Q, R \& S$ is/are :
questions.solution: $R$ and $S, P$ and $Q$ are resonating structures while $P$ and $R$ are tautomers structures.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following statements are correct for cis-1,2-dibromocyclopentane?
options: It contains two chiral centres, but is optically inactive
It can exist in two enatiomeric forms but cannot be optically active
It is with two chiral centres and is optically active
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which statement is incorrect about the following structures:
options: II has maximum van der waal strain between two vicinal hydrogen atoms at $\mathrm{C}_2-\mathrm{C}_3$ or $\mathrm{C}_5-\mathrm{C}_6$.
questions.solution: Cyclohexane in chair form is free from all strains. The boat form has maximum VDW strain between flagpoles hydrogen atom.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The correct statement(s) about the compound $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{HO}) \mathrm{HC}-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{CH}(\mathrm{OH}) \mathrm{CH}_3 ~\mathbf{(X)}$ is(are) :
options: The total number of stereoisomers possible for $X$ is 6 .
The total number of meso isomers possible for $X$ is 2 .
The number of fractions on fractional distillation of all stereoisomeric mixture of $X$ is 4 .
If the stereochemistry about the double bond in X is cis, the number of enantiomers possible for X is 2 .
questions.solution:
(X) has configurations
(A) R Z R
(B) S Z S
(C) R Z S
(D) R E R
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following represent(s) the enantiomer of $Y$ ?
questions.solution: Hint : The configuration of the stereocentre in $Y$ is ' $R$ ', while that of $A, B$ and $C$ are respectively $R, S$ and $S$. In option -D the configuration around the double bond is cis, so it can not be an enantiomer of $Y$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution: Availability of acidic $\alpha-H$ - atoms at $\left(^{*}\right)$ marked positions, enable the compounds to show ketoenol tautomerism.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Choose the correct statement(s) among the following.
options: $\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{CHO}$ on reaction with HCN gives racemic mixture
questions.solution:
These are cis and trans isomers not enantiomers (Thus, false).
(b) $CH_{3}CHO$ on reaction with HCN gives racemic mixture of cyanohydrin (Thus, true).
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
